questions.question: According to research, what is one of the most powerful predictors of mental health recovery and resilience?

options.text: High income
Strong social support
Living in a warm climate
Having a college degree

questions.explanation: Research consistently shows that strong social support is one of the most powerful predictors of mental health recovery and resilience. Having the right people in your corner can make the difference between success and struggle.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT one of the four types of support described in the lesson?

options.text: Emotional support
Financial support
Informational support
Practical support

questions.explanation: The four types of support described in the lesson are emotional support, informational support, practical support, and social support. While financial limitations are mentioned as a barrier, financial support is not listed as one of the four types.

questions.question: What is one common barrier to building a support system mentioned in the lesson?

options.text: Having too many friends
Being too healthy to need support
Stigma around mental health and fear of judgment
Not knowing what food to eat

questions.explanation: Stigma around mental health -- fear of judgment or misunderstanding -- is listed as one of the common barriers people face when building support networks.

questions.question: What does the lesson recommend as a first step for building support?

options.text: Post about your journey on social media
Hire a personal chef
Identify one person who might be supportive and share one small aspect of your journey
Join five different support groups at once

questions.explanation: The lesson recommends starting small by identifying one person who might be supportive and sharing one small aspect of your journey with them. This manageable first step builds momentum.

questions.question: According to the lesson, when is professional support essential?

options.text: Only when you cannot afford healthy food
When experiencing crisis situations such as thoughts of self-harm, severe depression, or panic attacks
Only after trying every other option first
Professional support is never necessary if you have friends

questions.explanation: Professional help is essential when experiencing crisis situations such as thoughts of self-harm or suicide, severe depression lasting weeks, panic attacks or severe anxiety, or inability to function in daily life. Seeking professional help is a sign of strength.
